name: Cynthia Leis
summary: AEDG President/CEO AND PAAC Executive Director — the single locus of control over the economic-development trade-secret shield and the records custody for Project BOSC.
body:
## How she appears in the record

Cynthia "Cindy" Leis occupies **two seats at once** in the Project BOSC corpus:

- **President/CEO of the Allen Economic Development Group (AEDG)** — she signs the
  Elida Local School District notice letters as AEDG President/CEO (`leisc@aedg.org`),
  and the PAAC board minutes track her rise from AEDG Business Development Director
  to President/CEO by August 2024.
- **Executive Director of the Port Authority of Allen County (PAAC)** — she signs the
  PAAC records policy in that capacity, and recurs throughout the board minutes
  reporting on the deal (e.g. 2026-03-30, tying the N. Cole Street roadway
  improvements to "the Google data center project").

## Why the dual role matters

PAAC has **no employees** of its own; it must be administered by an external entity
([records policy](../extracted/aedg/paac-records-policy.policy.yaml)). With Leis serving as
**both** AEDG President/CEO and PAAC Executive Director, the same individual sits on
both sides of the front-end developer-information shield (R.C. 4582.58 — developer
information routed *through AEDG* is treated as a non-record until the employer commits
in writing) **and** the records custody for the public authority that approves the
deal. That single locus of control over the econ-dev shield + records is the core of
the records-completeness concern. See the memory note `leis-aedg-paac-dual-role`.

In the board minutes she describes roundabout phasing to objecting residents
(2025-08-07), and the PAAC board invokes the R.C. 4582.58(B) trade-secret shield to
enter executive session (2026-03-30) — the shield in active use.

## Research status

Roles and the dual capacity are read directly from the cited extractions and are
**common-control plumbing and leads to verify — not** statements about beneficial
ownership or wrongdoing. To extend: confirm the exact AEDG/PAAC contractual
administration arrangement and the dates of each appointment from primary records.

name: Jill Tangeman
summary: Vorys counsel for Bistrozzi LLC — company counsel on the Roadwork Development Agreement, the land-option assignments, and a recurring contact of record across the EPA permit file.
body:
## How she appears in the record

Jill S. Tangeman is **Vorys, Sater, Seymour and Pease** counsel on the company
(Bistrozzi/BOSC) side, recurring across three threads:

- **Company counsel on the Roadwork Development Agreement** — named in the RDA notice
  block as company counsel (`jstangeman@vorys.com`)
  ([RDA](../extracted/aedg/roadwork-development-agreement.rda.yaml)).
- **The land-option assignments** — the PAAC purchase options (Brenneman, Neff, Pike
  Run/Miller) were **assigned to Bistrozzi LLC** through Vorys/Tangeman (2025-07-03),
  closing 2025-07-14 ([seller-land-packets](../extracted/aedg/seller-land-packets.land.yaml)).
- **Permit contact of record** — she appears as `permit_contact` on numerous EPA
  permit extractions (entity graph: permit_contact ×11).

She also appears (with Leis and Rex Huffman) in the Neff property **Conditional Use
Permit** process in the PAAC board minutes (2024-10-24).

## Why this matters

Tangeman and Scott Ziance are the two Vorys hands on the local deal paperwork: Ziance
on the CRA/NDA/SOS filings, Tangeman on the RDA and the land-option assignment chain.
Together they are the developer-side legal channel through which the land assembly and
the road/CRA instruments were executed.

## Research status

Roles are read from the cited extractions and are **common-control plumbing and leads
to verify — not** statements about beneficial ownership or wrongdoing. To extend:
confirm the assignment instruments' recording details and her signature blocks.
